question: A user task is deployed without a candidate user or group assignment in the BPMN. How can a user start working on it from Tasklist, and what if they change their mind?

options.text: Self-assign the task from the queue; if they no longer intend to work on it, they can unassign it.
The task is invisible to every user until an Operate operator runs a migration.
Only the BPMN author can claim it by re-editing the model in Modeler and redeploying with an assignee.
Tasklist auto-assigns the task to the first user who opens the Tasks page that day.

a.text: The docs state that user tasks can be automatically assigned in the BPMN process or self-assigned from Tasklist, and the user can unassign the task if they do not intend to work on it.

b.text: Unassigned tasks are visible in the Tasks page so users can pick them up; they are not hidden until an operator intervenes.

c.text: Assignment happens at runtime through Tasklist or the API; redeploying the model is not required to claim a task.

d.text: Tasklist does not silently auto-assign on first open; users must self-assign explicitly when the BPMN didn't assign anyone.

explanation: Tasklist supports two assignment paths: automatic assignment defined in the BPMN process (to a user or group) and self-assignment by the user from the Tasks page. A self-assigned task can also be unassigned if the user later decides not to work on it, putting it back into the queue for someone else.
